vulnerability.title: Install fapolicyd Package
vulnerability.description: The fapolicyd package can be installed with the following command: $ sudo dnf install fapolicyd
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if ! rpm -q --quiet "fapolicyd" ; then dnf install -y "fapolicyd" fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: The remediation involves a simple package installation via dnf. Installing fapolicyd is a standard hardening step and does not inherently modify existing system configurations or disrupt connectivity upon installation.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: Potential for application execution issues if fapolicyd is enabled and not properly configured for existing custom binaries

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan directly addresses the vulnerability by checking for the existence of the package and installing it if missing. This is the standard and correct approach for this remediation requirement.
suggested_improvements: After installation, the service should be enabled and started (e.g., 'systemctl enable --now fapolicyd') to ensure the security policy is actually enforced.
